summary: Completed comprehensive testing of PULSE real-time insights platform after major restructuring. Backend APIs working perfectly (100% success rate). Frontend UI and navigation working well with minor chart rendering issues.

ui_bugs.issue: Chart width/height rendering warnings in console - charts showing -1 dimensions
ui_bugs.details: Console shows: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' - affects visual presentation but doesn't break functionality

integration_issues.issue: One CORS/resource loading error in News tab
integration_issues.details: Console shows: 'Failed to load resource: net::ERR_BLOCKED_BY_RESPONSE.NotSameOrigin' - minor issue not affecting core functionality

passed_tests: Home page loads correctly at / with PULSE branding
Hero title and CTA button present and functional
Features section with 6 feature cards
Data sources section visible
Home theme toggle working (data-testid=home-theme-toggle)
Navigation from home to dashboard via CTA
Dashboard loads at /dashboard with PULSE branding
Search bar correctly removed (not present)
KPI cards displaying correctly (14 card elements found)
Anomaly detection panel present and showing 5 outliers
Export CSV button present and clickable (data-testid=export-button)
Home link navigation working (data-testid=home-link)
Dashboard theme toggle working (data-testid=theme-toggle)
All 4 platform tabs present: YouTube, Social, Entertainment, News
YouTube tab with 5 nested sub-tabs: Overview, Engagement, Categories, Growth, Competitive
All YouTube sub-tabs clickable and loading content
Social tab showing Twitter trends data (MOCKED as expected)
Entertainment tab showing TMDB + Spotify data
News tab showing articles with sentiment
All navigation flows working correctly
Theme toggles working on both home and dashboard
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix chart rendering issues - charts showing -1 width/height dimensions causing console warnings. This is likely a CSS/container sizing issue with Recharts components. Also investigate the CORS error in News tab resource loading.
